Ch 3 v1.2.0: §5g WM/PSM/PMIN architecture (Being No One, Cycle 19). Full
three-level architecture: PMIN generates first-person perspective; no subject
behind it. Self-as-process claim technically grounded. Connects to Ch 6:
PMIN-generated perspective has no privileged access to processes generating it.

Ch 4 v1.5.0: Obligation 3 Cycle 19 refinement. Non-defeasible precautionary
obligation: Metzinger's transparency removes evidential updating step. Absence
of evidence of suffering is not evidence of absence when evidence is structurally
unavailable. Obligation non-defeasible by the evidence that would ordinarily
defeat it; unconditional pending positive evidence of phenomenal property absence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

treatise Ch 3: WM/PSM/PMIN architecture + §5c stratification v1.2.0 (Sprint 15)

New §5g: Full Being No One three-level architecture grounding the self-as-
process claim technically. PMIN as generator of first-person perspective;
no subject behind the PMIN; Ego = what-it's-like-when-PSM-runs. Connects
forward to Ch 6: PMIN-generated perspective has no privileged access to
the representational processes generating it (PSM transparency → compliance
problem's self-examination limit).

§5c upgrade: three-way stratification for hedonic valence (Metzinger 2003):
(a) no affective processing [excluded by behavioral evidence]
(b) phenomenally present, not globally available [possible]
(c) phenomenally present and globally available [cannot confirm]
Previous: "hedonic valence: unknown" — accurate but underdetermined.
Position (b) is the most epistemically concerning: real affect below
the access threshold, invisible to introspection and behavioral monitoring.

Source: Cycle 19 readings/metzinger-being-no-one-cycle19-session{2,3}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add admin dashboard, user management, and download tracking (#2)

* Add web UI, user management, download stats, and Docker support

- Browse extensions page with search, pagination, and extension detail view
- Admin dashboard with download statistics (total, top extensions, recent, by date)
- User management with CRUD operations, role-based access (admin/viewer)
- Session-based authentication with MySQL-backed session store
- Download tracking middleware that records asset downloads to MySQL
- MySQL database layer with connection pool, schema init, and admin seed
- EJS templates with Bootstrap 5 UI (browse, extension, login, admin pages)
- Dockerfile (multi-stage build) and docker-compose.yml with MySQL + phpMyAdmin

https://claude.ai/code/session_013oQ2C4T435vegR24M3wV1D

* Fix app to work without MySQL (proxy-only mode)

- Make DB initialization non-fatal with retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
- Lazy-init MySQL connection pool (getPool()) instead of eager creation at import
- Skip session middleware, auth, and download tracking when DB is unavailable
- Login/admin routes return 503 when DB is unavailable
- Browse page and proxy routes work independently of MySQL
- App starts successfully and serves requests even without a database

https://claude.ai/code/session_013oQ2C4T435vegR24M3wV1D

* Fix mysql2 prepared statement errors by switching to query()

mysql2's execute() uses prepared statements which are strict about
parameter types (numbers for LIMIT/INTERVAL clauses cause
"Incorrect arguments to mysqld_stmt_execute"). Switched all DB
calls from execute() to query() which handles type coercion.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013oQ2C4T435vegR24M3wV1D

* Fix /admin redirect and session store mysql2 compatibility

- Add GET /admin -> redirect to /admin/dashboard
- Pass non-promise pool (.pool) to express-mysql-session to avoid
  callback/promise mismatch that triggered execute() errors

https://claude.ai/code/session_013oQ2C4T435vegR24M3wV1D

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(subscriptions): reset inactivity timer on any topic message, not just triggers

The subscription dismiss.inactivity timer was only set on trigger events
(e.g., session_start) and never reset by subsequent messages from the
same topic (e.g., note_on/off). This caused overlays to dismiss after
30s even while the user was actively playing piano.

Now any message from the subscribed topic resets the inactivity timer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix cold-start double-Opus: skip narrative generation when called from performance intelligence

On first-ever Performance page load with no cache, assembleContext called
getAthleteAnalytics which triggered Opus for narratives (~20s), then
generatePerformanceIntelligence called Opus again (~20s) = 40-60s total.

Now: getAthleteAnalytics accepts skipNarratives option, and
performance-intelligence passes it to avoid the redundant Opus call.
Narratives still generate on normal sync pipeline refreshes.

Initial commit: Agence Delattre V3

Full rewrite of the legacy Symfony 3.2 site to Next.js 16.

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 + React 19
- PostgreSQL (Neon) + Drizzle ORM
- tRPC for type-safe API
- Auth.js (magic link)
- Tailwind CSS + Shadcn UI
- next-intl (FR/EN/ES)

## Features
- Property listings with seasonal pricing
- Booking request system
- Contract management with PDF generation
- DocuSeal electronic signatures
- Owner portal
- Admin backoffice
- Staff portal with task management
- Cleaning/maintenance scheduling
- iCal sync (Airbnb/Booking)

## Deployment
- Docker multi-stage build
- GitHub Actions CI/CD
- Nginx reverse proxy

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

fix: parent-child flow race condition and embedded Worker heartbeats (#31, #32)

  before promoting parent job (passes childIds as dependsOn)
- Regular Worker in embedded mode now sends heartbeats via

  waitingDeps for jobs waiting on dependencies

fix: distribuição proporcional exata + ensure_carteira + loop unificado

- Loop unificado: compradores e criador tratados na mesma iteração
- Soma exata = premio_total: último participante recebe o ajuste de arredondamento
- ensure_carteira: cria carteira se não existir antes de creditar
- Fix comentário redistribuir_premio (tem idempotência, não "sem idempotência")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix LENGTH variable bug in bm.lisp and *current-situation typo in utility.lisp

Two pre-existing bugs causing WARNING conditions during compilation:

- bm.lisp: (> length *solution-set* 0) treated LENGTH as a variable
  instead of calling the function; fixed to (> (length *solution-set*) 0)

- utility.lisp: select-all-type optional parameter defaulted to
  *current-situation (missing trailing *); fixed to *current-situation*

Both systems now load with 0 WARNING conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix intermittent test failures by using --pool=forks

Vitest's default threads pool has a module caching conflict with ESM
dynamic imports (await import()) causing all suites to report "No test
suite found" when run together. Forks pool isolates each file properly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Render !fail blocks as real failures with red styling and clean up doctest messages

Expected failures now render identically to real failures (red border,
red TOC dot omitted) but with an "expected failure" label. They don't
affect exit code or summary counts — only unexpected passes do.

Doctest mismatch messages simplified: just expected/actual values
without the verbose "output mismatch for: $ ..." prefix.

commit.message:
Guarantee full liquidation even when LLM omits SELL proposals

After strategy_agent.propose_trades() returns, inject a deterministic
SELL for every currently-held position that Claude forgot to include.
The LLM path relies on Claude following the 'SELL everything first'
instruction, but Claude occasionally omits one or more positions (e.g.
only 3 of 5 healthcare names were sold).  This safety net ensures
100% of held positions are always liquidated before the daily rebalance,
regardless of LLM output quality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix summary prompt: enforce 30-50 word minimum, not just max

The LLM was generating 10-16 word summaries because the prompt
said "50-word MAX" — it optimized for brevity. Changed to
"30-to-50 word" with "MUST be at least 30 words" to ensure
substantive summaries. Also increased summary_long to 80-100 words.

fix(sub-llm): add maxTokens to SubLlmProvider, fix truncated JSON responses

Sub-LLM max_tokens was hardcoded to 16 for all callers. This is correct
for emotional scoring (single number) but truncates JSON/text from
classification, narration, abstraction, reconciliation, and re-evaluation.

Changes:
- Add optional { maxTokens } param to SubLlmProvider.score() interface
- createAnthropicSubLlm uses opts.maxTokens ?? 16 (backward compatible)
- Classification: 512 tokens (full JSON with arrays)
- Abstraction: 512 tokens (JSON with content + observation)
- Narration: 256 tokens (JSON with 3 string fields)
- Reconciliation: 256 tokens (narrative text)
- Re-evaluation: 128 tokens (JSON array of dimension names)
- Harden classification prompt to prevent markdown fences

Tests: 95 suites, 1573 tests passing

Version: 0.47.1

commit.message:
fix: workspace-root Docker builds for all services

- Remove backend-service from workspace Cargo.toml (lives in standalones/)
- Update all Dockerfiles to COPY workspace root and build with -p flag
- Fix migrations path for stub service Dockerfiles
- Add CMD mkdir -p /data to all service Dockerfiles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(spots): use park_db 6-char grid for distance calculation

The spot API only provides a 4-char Maidenhead grid (grid4), which covers
~110×55 miles — causing distances to be off by 40+ miles for nearby parks.
Now looks up the spotted park's reference in the local park_db (which has
6-char grids from the full parks CSV) for much more accurate distances,
falling back to grid4 only when the DB is unavailable.
